candidates.stmt:
Alan Hilliar is bringing something new to political life in Aldershot and Farnborough.

Alan is an experienced business change consultant and has worked with organisations around the country, helping them to use technology more effectively. He and his wife Carolyn live locally and are active in their local church in Ash. He regularly cycles to work in Farnham and enjoys walks with his wife Carolyn and their border collie, Rosie in the local countryside. Outside of work, he’s a talented singer and performs in local amateur musical theatre.

He’s lived and worked in this area for the past fifteen years and has put his energies into bringing about change in our local communities, working with young people, environmental groups and housing issues.

Alan says “The Conservatives have become obsessed with getting the UK out of the European Union at any cost. They have lost the plot and lost touch with the importance of international business, particularly in our local economy.”

“Labour has lost its purpose and direction. They recycle old ideas that no longer relate to the real issues this country is facing. They have no vision for our local community.”

“I want to see us stay within the European Single Market and to maintain the relationships that have helped us grow as an economy.”

“We need a fresh vision to put new life back into our town centres. We need a greater sense of community, pride and identity, celebrating the different traditions which have formed our values in order to build a society which is open, tolerant and free.”

Hello, I am a local veteran of 34 years of service, married to Babs (a Community NHS Nurse) with originally two daughters Pippa (Senior Engineer) and Georgie. We lost Georgie just before the pandemic but like so many miss her greatly.

I have been the County Councillor for Aldershot South for the last 4 years and have continued the great work of my predecessor in supporting the Residents, Schools, Care Homes, Charities and public services for North Town, Manor Park and Aldershot Park. Additionally, I have worked jointly with both County Departments and Rushmoor in supporting the regeneration programme of our town centre and housing areas.

I have also been supporting the residents in their fight against Crime and Anti Social Behaviour by liaising with both the Police, Charities and VIVID to make our estates more safe for both the elderly and young residents. I believe we must all play our part in making our communities a safer place to live in.

I am a great supporter of Climate Change, Country Parks and Nature Reserves, and any country sport that enables the public and our residents and families to get out and enjoy themselves. I am also a strong supporter of Tice Meadows Nature Reserve (TMNR) and have taken the Leader of Hampshire County Council to visit that reserve and meet the TMNR Group committee. Both the County and Rushmoor strongly support the retention of this GREEN area. To further the Climate Change cause, I am an active member of the Local Walking and Cycling Infrastructure Plans committee  (County & Rushmoor) to create and improve routes and pathways of Rushmoor to enable all elements of the public to use them and reduce vehicles on the road to key areas such as; schools, businesses, hospitals/Medical centres, shops and leisure centres.

I believe Hampshire to be the finest County in England, and that Aldershot has always played a major role in its history. Throughout the pandemic our NHS Staff, Armed Forces, Ambulance Drivers, Care Homes, Teac

With a decade of local government experience elsewhere, I returned to Hampshire, where I grew up and my paternal grandparents first arrived over eighty years ago. I have extensive experience of planning and of transport policy, enabling me to use highways evidence to resist or improve unsuitable development. I'm already a regular attendee at parish council meetings and undertake to support the great volunteers on our 16 parish councils as fully as I can, especially on improving our roads. As a mother of three boys, now adult, I bring experience of setting up a successful new school to the county councillor role of overseeing Hampshire's schools, 93% of which are outstanding or good.

I have lived in Ropley for over 25  very happy years. It has been a great place to bring up a family, work and enjoy so much of the fantastic aspects of Hampshire life.

While out talking to residents throughout the Alton Rural area, however, I hear the same frustrations and concerns about present life in Hampshire. People are fed up with the state of our roads and pavements, worry about further cuts to public services,  waiting times at GP surgeries and hospitals, and want to properly protect our precious environment.

For too long the Conservatives running Hampshire County Council have taken local residents' votes for granted. But the results of the District council elections and the near elimination of the Conservatives' 22,000 majority in the General Election show that people have rightly had enough.

We need a County Council that listens to you and acts genuinely on your behalf. A council where we can properly represent you and your interests so that you are part of making the decisions that affect us all. It's time for a change for the better in Hampshire: it's time for a Liberal-Democrat-led Hampshire County Council. Help me ensure Alton Rural is part of that change

David has lived in the Alton Rural Area for 33 years and takes a keen interest in the local countryside, footpaths and bridleways taking his dog for daily walks in the Meon Valley area.

He qualified as a mechanical engineer working in the midlands motor industry, from where he progressed into selling and exporting high value capital equipment
to the construction, cargo handling, shipping and ports industries. Spending time working and living in South East Asia.

On return to the UK he spent several years on secondment to the Dept of Trade and Industry. He has particular experience in company finance and business planning from being a commercial manager of a leading national franchise.

His views are very much that of applied Common Sense and a policy of what works in the real world. Hypocrisy in local and national politics should be routed out and stopped. His business experience gives him the skills to balance budgets with income and cut out unnecessary wasteful expenditure.

Below are some of the topics he would focus on if elected :

Policing – Local / neighbour hoods / Police Stations
Roads and Highways – Pot Holes
Housing Development - Brown Field sites versus green Belt
Social Care in the community
Education – 6 th form Colleges
Farming - grants and set aside, footpaths and bridle ways
Fly Tipping
David Burton - Reform UK Candidate - Alton Rural.

If elected, you can expect me to work for the interests of our communities as a whole, to champion every resident, to seek to build a sustainable future for generations to come, and to support residents today to be able to live their lives without fear, in strong communities that support each other, with local services that reliably support those communities.

If elected as your County Councillor for Alton Rural, I will champion the safety and well-being of everyone who lives, works, and travels in the division. I pledge to:

1. Fix our rural roads and bring back reliable buses and public transport links.
2. Protect our local services from bankruptcy cuts and Local Government Reorganisation.
3. Crack down on rogue traders to make our villages safer, and corporate vandalism to protect our green spaces.

live in Alton and I’m bringing up my children here. As a councillor on the District Council, I’m proud to have delivered improvements which make a real difference. That role has highlighted the significant impact that HCC has on the lives of local residents. I believe that Hampshire County Council is failing residents. It is failing our environment. It is failing to promote growth. Above all, its failure to manage its finances has led to unacceptable and drastic cuts in public services. I’m keen to use my professional skills and experience for the benefit of my neighbours - and play my part with (what I hope to be) a much enlarged group of Lib Dem councillors - to unlock the positive impact we believe HCC can have.

Alton born and bred, I know the town and people. I began work on the high street then started two flooring companies and now own a marine decking business with international clients.

As your councillor, I will fight waste, inefficiency and corruption in local government.   I am passionate about my home town, share your concerns and want to make Alton an even better place to live.

You deserve better representation and I am ready to listen.

Dear Neighbours,

Alton deserves honest communication, practical action, and a representative who will stand up for local residents.

This is a town I am proud to call home. It is a strong and welcoming community, and it is worth protecting what makes it special while improving the things that matter in everyday life.

The approach is simple: listen first, act promptly, and be honest about what can and cannot be done. People value clear communication, practical solutions, and someone who will follow through.

If elected, I will work closely with residents, local community groups, the Town Council, and fellow councillors to make sure local concerns are properly heard where decisions are made. Good representation means being visible, approachable, and accountable.

You should know you have someone who will take your concerns seriously, work hard on your behalf, and report back in plain English.

Alton deserves integrity, common sense, and action. Together, we can build a strong future for our community.

Yours sincerely,
Brighton Gono

If elected as your County Councillor for Alton Town, I will champion the safety and well-being of everyone who lives, works, and travels in the division. I pledge to:

-Sort out our traffic and make our streets safer. 
I will push for 20mph limits on residential roads, better crossings for pedestrians, and proper cycle routes to the station and our schools so families can choose to leave the car at home.
-Defend the services our town relies on. 
I will oppose any cuts to Alton Library and the Household Waste Recycling Centre, and I will fight for a fair funding deal from central government so our council does not go bankrupt on our watch.
-Support young people and families. 
I will champion affordable youth services, protect funding for our local schools, and work to make sure Alton has the activities and spaces our young people need to thrive.

Introducing Rachel Pattinson-West, your local Green Party candidate for Andover South in the upcoming Hampshire County Council elections. 🌱

Rachel is a mum, a charity worker and a cancer survivor. She is standing because she believes politics should work for everyone, not just the few.

She knows Andover. She sees what matters to people here and what the issues are.

🚰 Sewage being pumped into our rivers.
🍞 Families turning to food banks while Hampshire County Council cuts the support they depend on
🕳️ Roads full of dangerous potholes
🧩 SEND families waiting months for assessments

"People want to see their representatives following through with actions and not just making empty promises. They want to live in a place where buses run, roads are fixed and their families can enjoy their environment."

"Life can be crappy and complicated but politics shouldn't make it worse. I want to fight to make it better. So that we all have a nicer place to live and get to enjoy the important things in life."

For action, not excuses, vote Green on May 7th. 💚

To find out more: https://testvalley.greenparty.org.uk/local-elections-2026/andover-south/

#TestValleyGreens #GreenParty #LocalElections2026 #Hampshire #VoteGreen #Andover

I am incredibly proud to have the chance to stand again to become North West Hampshire's first Labour MP. North West Hampshire is my home, it is where I grew up and it has shaped who I am. I work across Andover, Whitchurch, Overton, Kingslcere and Tadley, there is not a square inch of this constituency that I do not know or do not want to see thrive.



You know, as well as I do, the challenges we face across North West Hampshire after 14 years of Conservative government. Our High Streets are emptying, our NHS is crumbling, our schools need improving, our neighbourhoods need proper policing, our Armed Forces need support and our rural communities have been cut off with buses slashed, libraries closed and banks disappearing.



There is hope for a better future though. There is hope if you vote for it.



A Labour Government led by Keir Starmer will transform our country and our communities. As your local MP, I will fight for our beautiful area to make sure we get the support we need to thrive once more.



We can see local police in our neighbourhoods again and schools that are thriving so every child is given the opportunity to succeed. We can see our NHS given the doctors, nurses and equipment it needs through a new NHS workforce plan and an expansion of new technology to diagnose and treat people more quickly. We can see Andover's High Street renewed and our small businesses supported by scrapping business rates and bringing in a fairer system. We can see Whitchurch and Overton have sustainable development with housing for the next generation alongside infrastructure support. We can see jobs in Tadley protected as we continue to support our Nuclear Deterrent. Across our beautiful villages, we can see rural crime tackled, the opportunity for new bus routes, banking hubs so we no longer have banking deserts.



We can get our future back, but only if you vote for it. Only I can beat the Tories in North West Hampshire.

As a 45-year resident of Test Valley now retired, I am deeply rooted in our community. I am standing to restore the values of well-being and a strong work ethic where hard work pays, ensuring families can thrive without the burden of
crippling taxes.
Beyond economics, my commitment is to our safety; I will fight for secure streets where our wives and daughters can walk freely without fear.
My campaign is built on protecting our environment and for the families who call this area home.

Since the early eighties I have been an active local campaigner and volunteer, serving as a councillor at parish, borough and county level and have 25 years' experience as a school governor and charity trustee. I enjoy listening to music, watching historical, natural world and science documentaries, researching the family history and spending time with my grandchildren.

What are my priorities?

Roads, Footpaths and access to recreational spaces Timely repairs of potholes, pavements and the cutting back of overgrown hedges and trees on footpaths.Improve the foot and cycle paths in and around Andover linking nature reserves, parks and green spaces.Accessible countryside and woodland paths for all including users of mobility scooters and wheelchairs.

Let's get serious about recycling - More recycling programmes to produce year-on-year increases in Test Valley's 37% recycling rate. Reduce the use of land fill and the incineration of 58% of Test Valley's waste. The introduction of household food waste collection and doorstep glass collection.

Education, every day a learning day - Working for a level playing field for low income and disadvantaged young people.Working with the third sector to provide education and leisure courses using community buildings. 

Joined-up thinking on Health and Social Care - End the postcode lottery, ensure people get the care and support they need with essential living activities. Invests in care workers to ensure high quality care and give increased support for unpaid family carers.

I’m standing to be your county councillor because I care deeply about our communities and want to make a real difference locally. I'm ready from Day 1 and with local government reorganisation well underway, residents need an experienced, able and strong voice.

As a local councillor, I’ve spent years working with residents, listening to concerns, and helping to resolve issues. If you ask anyone in my ward, they will tell you how hard I work for the community.

It's not just about slogans for me or empty promises or copying the same messaging which I've already been sharing! I will always work hard to represent residents and stand up for our area.

Baddesley resident Peter Lazic is standing to provide the straightforward, no-nonsense representation his community deserves.
Deeply connected to the area, Peter is committed to protecting local priorities and ensuring residents’ voices are heard at the decision-making table.
He brings a practical, common-sense approach to local issues, rooted in the values of honesty and reliability.
Peter is dedicated to standing up for his neighbours and delivering the clear, accountable leadership needed to support the home he cares for so profoundly.

Sean Dillow – Conservative Candidate for the Basingstoke Central Division.

On 7th May, you will be choosing who represents you at Hampshire County Council. Yes, it’s the first opportunity since the General Election for residents to have their say — but more importantly, it’s about electing a County Councillor who will work hard for Basingstoke.

I’m Sean Dillow — a Borough Councillor, family man, and proud Basingstoke resident for nearly 40 years. I’ve raised my family here and worked locally as a photographer for the Basingstoke Gazette. I’ve seen the very best of our town — and its challenges too.

Residents know me as a councillor who stands shoulder to shoulder with local people, asks difficult questions, and rolls up his sleeves to fight for the community.

Hampshire County Council is responsible for the services that affect our daily lives — roads and transport, adult social care, education, SEND provision and children’s services. If elected, I will work tirelessly to ensure these services deliver the very best for residents.

I will push for:

Safer roads, fewer potholes and proper resurfacing

High-quality care for elderly and vulnerable residents

Strong schools and better SEND support

More free parking to support independent businesses

An infrastructure-first approach to new housing, ensuring community facilities come first

I am supporting residents impacted by the SNG regeneration proposals, stood with the Down Grange community to protect Down Grange Park, and continue to back the campaign for Local Nature Reserve status for Old Down Woodland Park.

I believe residents deserve a councillor who challenges underperformance, demands value for money, and fights for better outcomes.

If you want a County Councillor who campaigns, supports the community, and speaks up for residents —

Vote Sean Dillow on 7th May.

Sajish is a long time resident of Popley and works in the NHS Hospital in Basingstoke. He is a big asset to the local community and has worked on committees for St Bede’s Church and the Basingstoke Multicultural Society.  Sajish is worried about crime and antisocial behaviour and would like to see a Neighbourhood Watch scheme covering the whole ward.

Arun is a proud Basingstoke resident, father of two, provides information and technology consultancy services to a Ministry of Defense supplier. Having lived in Basingstoke over the last 20 years, he has gained a deep understanding and appreciation of the needs of this ever growing community. This has enabled his ability to work collaboratively within the community. 

ARUN’S COMMUNITY ACTIVITY: 
	As a Covid community hub lead, helped several vulnerable people 
	Ran a community kitchen project and delivered over 5000 food to our NHS staff
	As a lead organizer of “NokidSleepsHungry” program delivered food for needy families
	As a Royal Voluntary Service member, while volunteering at the Fire Station vaccination center he adds provision of hot food to vaccination staff and other volunteers 
	Have successfully ran one of the flag ship events of Basingstoke “World party” in the park for five years 
	Delivered free Information Technology classes for primary school children
	Led community safety campaign and conducted awareness surgeries with local police and community.  

ARUN’S COMMUNITY ROLES: 
	School Governor – Kings Furlong Junior School.
	Trustee – Basingstoke Voluntary Action.
	Trustee & Former Chair – Basingstoke Multicultural Forum.
	Trustee – Brookvale Community Association 
	Committee Member- Basingstoke Interfaith.
	Committee Member – Basingstoke Insight.
	Member – Basingstoke and Deane District Police Independent Advisory Group. Hampshire Constabulary. 

AWARDS & RECOGNITION:
	Recipient of Destination Basingstoke's 'Volunteer of the year' award 
	Special recognition award winner by Hampshire Police and Crime Commissioner 
	Invited to 10 Downing Street London in recognition of his community services
	BBC South recognized and featured his activities in news. 

Arun is a hard- working, committed community activist with a truly remarkable and exemplary ability to bring people together and drive change. He is an award-wi

Angie is currently a local councillor for Winklebury, and is looking forward to the challenges representing a wider community as the ward extends out to include the Manydown and more rural area. she is a committed councillor and campaigner, having fought hard on local issues like saving the Fort Hill School, and is now working to make sure residents views are heard in the development of the local area and that the whole ward benefits from the development of community facilities in Manydown.

Andy is a full-time software specialist. With his fiancée Janet, he has a busy and active life.  He is a well respected member of the Church of Christ the King and Editor of the Brighton Bell, and set up the Brighton Hill Neighbourhood Watch. As well as being Leader of the Basingstoke Labour Councillors Group, Andy is well known for supporting residents with their issues and fighting hard for improvements for the Brighton Hill, including parking improvements, the Brighton Hill Roundabout and local leisure and sport facilities. He is keen to make sure the council supports our local businesses and also provides top quality services to residents without continually increasing charges for basics such as new and replacement bins, and our bulky and garden waste services.

Inequality is at the heart of much that's wrong with society. It pits struggling people against each other while those with the most hoard wealth and power. In Hatch Warren and Beggarwood I will:

•	Bring my INDEPENDENT voice to issues of environmental protection, land development and infrastructure; •	PROTECT Old Down and our green spaces for future generations; •	Hold the new Beggarwood Surgery provider to ACCOUNT; •	LISTEN to residents, WORK tirelessly for them and FIGHT for their community without fear or favour.

WE are a fresh, collaborative force in British politics uniting people of all backgrounds to fight for equality, so that everyone benefits. WE fight for:

- Equality in Politics and Business - Equal Pay - Equality in Education - Equal Parenting and Caregiving  - Equal Media Treatment - Equality in Healthcare - Ending Violence Against Women & Girls

Why WE?

•	WE bring an equality lens to every facet of local government: representing everyone regardless of their wealth, gender, race, sexuality or class; •	An independent voice: no donors, unions, party structures or traditions getting in the way of scrutiny and action; •	A truly collaborative approach, taking the tribalism and power games out of politics.

I will be a strong voice on the Council for the people of Hatch Warren & Beggarwood because I will answer to them alone.

Jonathan Williams has lived all his life in Bishop’s Waltham.  For nine years he worked in the NHS, on the front-line in A&E at Winchester hospital, and for five years volunteering as a Community First Responder. 

Since retraining at Solent University as a software developer, he has worked creating digital interactive training for the Royal Navy, and now works as a Senior Developer creating digital training for overseas Doctors, Paramedics, and the NHS.

Jonathan has been a proven local campaigner over the years, working with Councillors to try to prevent further flooding in our area, successfully campaigning to prevent the gas power plant development and continued campaigning against the Shedfield quarry, and reporting multiple defects such as pot holes, dangerous and overgrown council owned foliage, and broken parking meters.

He wants to see safer speed limits on our approach roads to Bishop's Waltham, supports the national 20's plenty campaign for residential streets, a renewed priority on our local infrastructure to accommodate the recent new developments on the south of Bishop's Waltham as well as protect the green gaps we have in Durley from expansions of Horton Health and Boorley Green.

Jonathan is a proven local campaigner, and personally wants to put something back into the community by representing local residents on Winchester City Council.  If elected, he can achieve so much more and provide that crucial link between residents and their City Council.

Dear Fellow Resident,
It has been my privilege to serve as your Councillor for the Upper Meon Valley ward over the past four years.  Many of you know me well by now and know I have put my heart and soul into representing your interests and helping find solutions to local problems.  I have greatly enjoyed working across the ward and would very much like to continue, seeking re-election, as well as stand for Hampshire County Council, in the Bishop’s Waltham Division.
For those residents less familiar with me, I am a science graduate with a 30 year business career, living locally in Bramdean with my wife and family.  I am committed to using that experience to deliver practical solutions to the issues we all face - potholes, flooding, sewerage, speeding and noise, protecting our countryside and supporting our rural businesses while spending your council tax wisely.
I believe in practical action and when Bishop’s Waltham tip faced possible closure, I met with Cllr Nick Adams-King, leader of Hampshire County Council, to stress its importance, helping secure its future.
There are many challenges ahead, but I care deeply about our community and will continue working hard to make our area an even better place to live and work.  I hope you will support me on 7th May.
If you would like to raise any issue, then please do get in touch.
With very best wishes
Neil

I live in Twyford with my young family and have spent over 18 years working in humanitarian aid and international development. I am driven by a commitment to making a positive difference and a proven ability to get things done.

I spent six years working closely with communities in Sudan for a British charity, supporting people to come together and develop local, community-led solutions to the devastating impacts of climate change.

Last year, I worked tirelessly during a short by-election campaign to win a seat on Winchester City Council, something many thought was not possible.

The collaborative, listening-based approach I developed in Sudan now guides my work as a councillor. I work with residents in Colden Common and Twyford to help shape local priorities and build a more resilient future. I am keen to bring this same community-first approach to communities across Bishop’s Waltham and the Upper Meon Valley, giving more people a stronger voice to drive local change.

Over the past year, it has been rewarding to work alongside local campaigners to push for better bus services, better housing, safer roads, and improved travel for cyclists and mobility users, while holding river polluters to account. However, much of the decision-making power sits with Hampshire County Council. That is why I am standing to have a greater influence on the decisions that affect our community.

Just like last year, we believe a positive result is possible, but it will only happen with your support.

Representing people, not a party

“Do you feel like I do? That political  parties are not listening to the concerns of local people? That they have lost touch with the wishes of Bishopstoke? 

With a background in local government and a masters degree I felt it was time to give back to my community. These concerns inspired me to stand up for Bishopstoke & Fair Oak. I've been privileged and proud to have received so much support from my local community, now currently representing local people on Hampshire County Council (since 2022) Eastleigh Borough Council (Since 2018) and Bishopstoke Parish (since 2016). successfully run for the Parish Council election in 2016. Together with the local community and my Independent colleagues I have helped to convince the Local Planning Inspector that the potentially 5000+ buildings proposal in the north of our villages would be destructive and I am now actively seeking the same area be protected as a nature reserve. After three years of campaigning for safer indication of the junior school and slower speeds, I was thrilled, that within my first 6months at Hampshire, warning lights and a 20mph limit has been installed. I  have sought to influence decision makers. 

Including: speaking up on behalf of residents at local meetings; helping residents experiencing difficulties with developments; challenging policies on fly-tipping; and, pushed for new footpaths. Bishopstoke has always been home. I  went to the local Infant and Junior school,  I’m a member of Bishopstoke Players  and Bishopstoke History Society. I grew up with its ancient woodland & Itchen and want to see these preserved for future generations”

If elected I will: ✓ continue to fight eco-vandalism that could devastate our ancient woodland, countryside and the Itchen  ✓ keep local people informed of the decisions that will affect us and listen to their views ✓ support services and policies which contribute to the health and wellbeing of local people, our environment and wi

After nine years of military service, I ran a successful business for over 30 years while continuing to serve the community as a special constable, retained firefighter, and community first responder.

As a parish councillor in neighbouring Allbrook, I remain committed to practical action and strong local representation.

My wife and family grew up locally, and through years of volunteering I have built lasting connections across the area. I understand the challenges residents face — protecting vital services, improving infrastructure, and keeping our community safe, welcoming, and thriving.

I believe in listening first, working collaboratively, and delivering real results that benefit everyone. I am community-focused and dedicated to putting residents before politics.

Fair Oak and Horton Heath’s longest serving councillor, Nick was Mayor of the Borough in 2023/2024. He is also Vice Chair of the Fair Oak and Horton Heath Parish Council. He led the team that rescued Fair Oak Library from County Council cuts and is an adviser at Citizens Advice. Nick enthusiastically supports the Borough Council’s One Horton Heath project which is on course to provide 2500 much-needed houses at no cost to council taxpayers. He also praises the development of Stoke Common Park. As a Saints season ticket holder, he has recently started to believe in miracles.

My name is Ben Burcombe-Filer, and I am the Conservative candidate for Southampton Test in this general election. 

My journey to this point has always had Southampton Test at its heart. I first came to Southampton Test aged 18 months having been diagnosed with cerebral palsy and written off. My fightback began with the skill help and support of Southampton General, who have been there every step of the way since. 

“My whole life, professional and personal, has been about overcoming challenges, in environments where failure simply is not an option. I hope to be able to use those attributes in serving and representing the residents of Southampton Test. Together we can ensure bold action provides a Secure future for the City.”

My main aims if elected as MP would be

1.	To ensure the people of Southampton Test feel that their voice is heard and they are represented. With a Labour MP since 1997 and a council on which 70% of the seats are held by Labour there is no check or debate currently. The people deserve an MP who will listen, not just hear, and act accordingly. 

2.	Focus on the things that really matter to people. Continuing to cut crime and antisocial behaviour, cleaning up our streets, further elevating the pride Southampton communities feel in their city. 

3.	Ensure the resilience of infrastructure and industry in the city, making sure the port has the capacity to continue to provide quality jobs for residents for years to come by helping to  attract further investment. As well as making sure our infrastructure and transport network is future proofed so the people and business’ of Southampton Test can plan with confidence and thrive.  

I feel strongly that I have a debt to the people and place of Southampton Test, without them I simply would not be here. Now I want to use my skills and experiences to give back.

I am proud to stand for Bishopstoke, where I have lived for over 13 years and where my children attend local schools. I work in a local college, so I understand the importance of education, opportunity and strong community support. I care deeply about the area and the people who live here. I want to protect our environment, support local services, and ensure residents have a real voice in decisions that affect them. I will work hard, listen to concerns, and stand up for Bishopstoke every day and always put our community first in everything I do for local residents.

Rupert was elected to the Borough Council in 2002 and is Botley’s longest-serving councillor. As Cabinet Member at Eastleigh for the environment, Rupert has championed recycling, air and water quality, and drainage concerns.  A past Mayor, he is also a Parish and County Councillor. Rupert led the campaign for Botley’s by-pass, opposed for many years by the County Council, and has recently won his campaign to get pavements along more of Kings Copse Avenue. Next to get the speed limit down! He is working to secure more doctors’ appointments and facilities at Botley Surgery, and a pharmacy at Boorley.

I’m standing as a Green candidate because I care deeply about Botley and Hampshire, where I was raised and have lived locally for seven years. I understand the issues residents face and believe politics should prioritise listening, transparency, and long-term wellbeing over short-term profit. My priorities include protecting green spaces, supporting strong communities, and ensuring development benefits residents without straining infrastructure. I support sustainable planning, improved public transport, safer walking and cycling routes to reduce congestion and pollution. I am committed to addressing the cost-of-living crisis through affordable housing and community initiatives. I will represent residents with integrity and dedication.

I’m a local business owner, but more importantly, I’m your neighbour. Like many of you, I care deeply about what happens in our community, and I don’t think our area is getting the focus or representation it deserves.

For years, Eastleigh Borough Council has been dominated by one party with very little meaningful opposition. That lack of balance isn’t healthy for anyone.

As a business owner, I’m particularly concerned about the level of council debt – among the highest in the country – while residents continue to face rising council tax. Too often, large sums of public money are spent without enough scrutiny or challenge.

If you’re looking for someone who will challenge the current leadership, question how your money is spent and stand up for our community, I’d be grateful for your support.

STEVE HAMMOND has been the Councillor for Bridgemary North since 2017. He lives within the Bridgemary North Ward and is married with two adult children and one grandchild. A Gosport resident since 1962 Steve worked in the telecommunications sector and is now retired. Steve is a member of St. Mary’s junior sailing club where he teaches 9-15 year old children to sail. He is also a member of the Lords Taverners where he has helped disabled children play table cricket. Steve also hosts 10 Facebook business pages where local firms can promote their businesses for free.

Living in Bridgemary and running a small family business gives me a chance to communicate with everyone and learn every day. I have helped my community in tough times by cooking for families and vulnerable members of the community. I am always keen to help all residents where I can. I understand the issues in our local area, am passionate and aim to work hard for Bridgemary North helping residents with my fresh ideas.

(1) challenging wasteful spending and demanding proper scrutiny of council finances;

(2) contesting decisions that increase costs for residents without improving services; and

(3) focusing spending on core services that people in Eastleigh rely on.

At work, I deal with complex financial issues. I would bring that same discipline locally, holding decision-makers to account and standing up for residents facing rising costs and declining value.

I have lived in Eastleigh for the past decade and am proud to call it home. I’m standing in this by-election because I believe our town deserves better. I also support Labour’s plans to reform local government. Replacing the borough and county council structures with a single unitary authority and a directly elected mayor would mean clearer accountability, better value for money, and stronger leadership for our community.

I’m passionate about making a real difference for Eastleigh; I hope I can count on your support.

Your Voice. Your Community. Your Money.

Open and Straight answers.
Evidence-based decisions.

At HCC cabinet level, Steve supported securing additional pothole repair resources mid-winter and the new lane-rental disruption charge which is expected to raise up to £5m per year extra towards highway maintenance.

Fleet is a fantastic town, but residents increasingly feel infrastructure and services are struggling to keep up with change. I have lived in Fleet for 24 years and have been part of the local area for over 50 years.
Professionally I work in the technology sector leading international teams and managing risk in critical environments. I believe local government should focus on practical decisions that protect residents, support local businesses, and maintain the quality of life that makes Fleet such a great place to live for everyone in our community.
I live locally with my family and am committed to protecting Fleet’s character for future generations.

Protect Fleet’s Character
Fleet has seen significant housing growth over the past decade. Future development must only proceed when infrastructure, roads, schools and GP services can properly support it, so that our town remains a great place to live.

Fix Roads and Infrastructure
Residents have seen a noticeable decline in the condition of local roads and pavements over the past year. I will press Hampshire County Council to prioritise repairs and ensure Fleet receives its fair share of infrastructure investment.

Revitalise Fleet Town Centre
Fleet deserves a thriving town centre. I want to support and attract independent shops, restaurants and local businesses that make the High Street somewhere residents enjoy visiting again and that better serves the needs of our residents.

Many of our residents in Fleet are struggling to get by. Rising energy, food, and fuel prices have left people working harder and paying more, but getting less. Services desperately need improving. We need a local voice with strong values to make sure our Council steps up. Vote for Andrew Perkins

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt: I am standing as a Green Party candidate for Hamble as the only progressive, inclusive and diverse party to oppose Reform UK policies. I support the Green Party's policies to improve workers' rights, nationalise water companies, abolish tuition fees for low-income university students and raise the minimum wage. I support working from home to reduce pollution and congestion. Working from home for people with disabilities and childcare responsibilities reduces stress and anxiety which improves mental health in staff, and in the long run will boost staff morale.

Worked in IT for 40+ years , in the public and private sector. A qualified economist and systems implementation specialist . Also an member of Greenpeace since 1985, as well as the Green Party here in Hampshire and in Cheshire Moved to Gosport last year and believe that we have four areas we need to tackle 1. A more equal distribution of the rewards of our economic system. This will reduce inequality and opens up opportunity for everyone to succeed. There is plenty of research that shows making a society more equal makes it a happier place 2. Finland was ranking the happiest country in the world in 2023 -- why ? because their welfare system's ability to help its citizens feel taken care of," Things like relatively generous unemployment benefits and nearly free healthcare help mitigate sources of unhappiness, 3. Push for the move to a greener economy to tackle climate change AND give us cleaner energy , reduced pollution making our environment cleaner and quieter and remove the need for dirty fossil energy , aiming for a safe and healthy Gosport 4. I heard a commentator on the radio today say 'we can't afford Net Zero' -- really we need to wake up Gosport to because the costs of not doing Net Zero area LOT higher If I had to have a slogan, it would be ' Doing Nothing is not an option -- for you , me and 9 billion others on planet Earth -- we need to start here ..

lived in Hampshire all his life
Opposed to over development specifically murrell green and Firgrove road
Strong supporter that utilities should do all road repairs after they have finished road works
Hartley Wintney recycling centre threatened closure, Tim ensured it remained open
Had clear important new road signs put in place

I am a Qualified Accountant with over 40 years business and industry experience. I am committed to cutting and eliminating all waste to deliver the best value for money for your Council tax. I commit to be a strong voice being accessible and responsive not ignoring requests for help as many experience now. We at Reform oppose rip off Car park charges which hurt our local retailers. We will work hard with the police to fight shoplifting and all anti social behaviour.
I have knocked on over 700 doors so far and will continue to do so until election day listening to the issues concerning you. Have the other parties bothered to talk to you ??
 Vote for change on May 7th vote for  Dave Lovell Reform Uk

Hayling Island is special. And for too long, it has been ignored.

Overlooked by the Borough. Overlooked by the County. While other areas got attention, Hayling got left behind.

I have lived and worked here for over 18 years, but Hayling has been in my heart since first visiting 35 years ago. This island is my home, my community, my passion. And that is exactly why I am fighting for it.

Two years as your Councillor has shown me what is possible when someone actually stands up for Hayling. Now I want to do more.

My priorities:

🌊 Deliver the replacement sea defences at West Beach and the South Hayling Sea Defence Scheme

💧 Hold Southern Water to account over continuous sewage discharges

🚧 Take real action on the pothole epidemic

🚗 Properly scrutinise traffic control on our main access road

🏡 Continue fighting to stop major development on the Island

Two years ago I made you a promise. My record shows I kept it. Now I am asking for the chance to go further, fight harder, and make sure Hayling Island finally gets the voice it deserves.

Vote Jonathan Hulls. For Hayling. For all of us.

Tonia is Bursledon’s longest-serving Borough Councillor and is Cabinet Member for Health at the Borough Council, leading on working with the NHS to improve our local health services and on community safety. She is also a member of the County Council where one of her roles is holding the Police & Crime Commissioner to account. Last year she was asked by Hound Parish Council to take on chairing the Parish Council that covers Butlocks Heath in her Borough ward. Tonia is pressing the County Council to tackle potholes and the dreadful condition of Hamble Lane and other local roads.

Dave has lived in West End/Eastleigh Borough for over 20 years. He is a hardworking, community-minded candidate who has worked for the NHS since 2002 as a senior software engineer for University Hospital Southampton. He has served on the board of a number of local charities and chaired Eastleigh Green Party. Dave is the proud father of two (almost) grown-up boys and is passionate about building a sustainable, inclusive, and hope-filled society for them and future generations. He is also a musician, a YouTube content creator and has a keen interest in local history and heritage.

Jackie has stood up for residents on local councils for over 20 years. She has helped hundreds of people and works tirelessly to help our communities in The Worthys, Worthy Down, and Kings Barton to thrive. Jackie is Chairman of the Tubbs Hall, a Governor at Kings Worthy School, and a trustee of The Watercress Way.

Born in Winchester and living in the Worthys and Itchen Valley since 2018 I combine over 35 years of business experience with a strong record of local service. I am a parent of two, a charity trustee, parish councillor and former school governor. I bring real life experience, practical judgement and a deep commitment to supporting residents.

Graham and his wife Mal have lived in Lee on the Solent for over 40 years. He moved here with his family whilst serving in the Royal Navy where he served for 24 years both above and below the waves. Graham has been a Gosport Borough Councillor since 1998 but most recently worked closely with fellow Councillors, local organisations and volunteers to make LEE HUB a reality, ensuring that we do not lose our library.

I have lived in the ward, with my young family, since 2014. In that time I have been a proud member of the community, leading local events and supporting causes, all the while listening to residents’ concerns. My background is in IT solutions for charities and I intend to use that experience to serve Grange and Alver Valley as its councillor.

We have a fantastic piece of Gosport here. The beautiful Alver Valley on our doorstep, and the most genuine people you could hope to meet. We are also unfairly overlooked when it comes to handing out funding, which is why I got involved in local politics. For over a decade our representatives have done a poor job in standing up for our area and it is time that changed.

Gosport Labour is on your side. We rise above childish political games to deliver on the priorities of Gosport’s residents.

Living in Gosport all my life and working over 20 years within the Education sector across the Hampshire region, I am invested and committed to a thriving, safe and greener community. A strong society where communities can improve the quality of their lives, and thriving businesses create local jobs, with good infrastructure, social care and training to ensure skills for jobs.  I am seen as being honest, hardworking and someone who really listens.

I will be a strong voice for Gosport, conducting myself with integrity, dedication and honesty. I will ensure that Gosport is given all the support available to enable our area to reach our full potential. Few and far between I am passionate about protecting our green spaces. I will challenge inequality & defend our hometown from unsympathetic development. Ensuring accessibility, I will always be visible and contactable to help residents with their issues and concerns.

ROB HYLANDS has been a Brockhurst Ward Councillor since 2009 and is currently the Deputy Leader of the Liberal Democrat Group. Married to Lorna, Rob is the landlord of The Foresters Arms public house in Anns Hill Road, Gosport. He enjoys most sports from darts to football and is a keen supporter of Gosport Borough FC. In his spare time he enjoys walking his two small dogs and supporting local community organisations and events.

Julie was born and grew up in Gosport.  She raised her family here and her daughter went to local schools. Julie moved away for a few years, but returned to Gosport in 2016 to be close to her family (and the seaside!) again.  Since being elected in 2018, Julie has worked tirelessly for her area. Supporting residents with casework, organising local litter picks, and lobbying the council for improvements to the Kings Road play area.  Julie is fed up with the way the Conservative-run government is taking us for granted, with sleaze, rising fuel and food costs, and MPs partying at Downing Street during lockdown whilst everyone else followed the rules.  As a Governor for a local school, Julie knows how hard teaching staff work, and how hard Conservative-run government cuts have hit schools. They give money with one hand, but take more away with another.  Julie believes the Lib Dems truly stand up for local people, and that is why she is standing for council this year.  Please elect Julie Westerby on May 5th.

Lucy has lived in Gosport her entire life, and she recently joined the Green Party last year after becoming disillusioned with the way that the current Labour government has been handling things. She believes that the Green Party has renewed hope in politics, not just for her, but for so many people, and she wants to be a part of that movement. Crucially, they put people and the environment at the centre of everything they do, and value life over shareholder profits. Some of their policies that she is passionate about include:
- Bringing the railways back into public ownership 
- Investing in green energy and phasing out fossil fuels 
- Protecting our local nature and wildlife 
- Taxing the ultra wealthy

Hilary has a long association with Grange, regularly gets involved in conservation projects and runs a children’s environmental group from Grange Farm 1990. She continues to work in Early Years education and organises collections for the local foodbank.

Conservative cuts mean that Gosport Council gets 40% less funding than it did in 2010. The evidence is everywhere: from schools to street lights, from play parks to policing, libraries closed, fewer flower beds, streets cleaned less often. Like most residents, she is especially concerned about the recent rise in crimes, such as drug-dealing and anti-social behaviour.

I am honoured to have been selected to stand as the Conservative Candidate for Hampshire County Council Liphook, Headley, and Grayshott Division election on May 6th.

My husband, Peter and I moved to Bramshott in 2011 from Odiham with our 4 rescue cats. I am a “Hampshire Hog” with ancestors traced back to the early 1800’s. I have many years of business and client management experience in the IT and Communications Industry with expertise and qualifications in marketing, project delivery and services management. During my IT career I have been involved in helping Public Sector organisations, amongst others, to deliver innovative IT solutions to bring new services their customers. 

I have also volunteered for charities and causes including the RSPCA, Cats Protection and most recently was a founding member of the Liphook Help Hub created to help our vulnerable and isolating neighbours during the COVID-19 lock down.

The value I bring is ‘I deliver’. 

I am an active Bramshott and Liphook Parish Councillor, sitting on the Recreation Committee and as a team member of several Working Parties. I am very concerned about speeding through the villages and have lobbied my fellow Councillors for traffic calming measures.  We can look forward to traffic calming gates in Bramshott later this year and wildfowl and deer warning signs to protect our wild animals on our roads. I am really looking forward to supporting the Parish Council Liphook Speed Watch. 

I am passionate about conservation, animal welfare and creating a green environment. If elected I will work to

•	Ensure continued Road and Traffic improvements
•	Protect and enhance our green spaces.
•	Increase opportunities for recycling.

and help to reduce speeding, tackle rural crime and respond to climate change.

Working together with Councillors in neighbouring Divisions, I will lobby for affordable, accessible local transport for people living in areas not served by a regular bus service.

“Having ta

I have lived in Bramshott near Liphook for 30 years and have both children and grandchildren living close by.

I consider myself a ‘small c’ conservative, having spent a lifetime in business rather than politics. I started and managed businesses (including a local business that employed 30 people) and worked for major corporations both in the UK and abroad, so I understand about managing people and resources, and being prudent with finances.

I was driven to support Reform through desperation at the failure of recent governments – both the last Conservative and current Labour one. I believe in small government, low taxes and high growth. The Conservatives and Labour have delivered the opposite of these things.

So I was delighted to be asked to stand for Reform in the local elections. Listening to local concerns, the number 1 issue in the area is the continued threat of substantial new housing developments without the related investment in infrastructure. National housing targets imposed by central government have led to large housing developments being waived through by the local Conservative authorities despite their talk of protecting rural communities. People feel they have no say in what is happening.

In 2019, The Conservative-run Hampshire County Council declared a ‘Climate Emergency’ – who voted for that? Since then, they have spent and estimated £40m on ‘Net Zero’ initiatives. Their planning policies typically prioritise supposed ‘climate change mitigation over whether local people actually support the proposed development. At the same time, our roads are congested, full of potholes, flood regularly (witness the semi-permanent ‘great flood of Bramshott’) and are often strewn with litter (left to be addressed by heroic local litter-picking residents).

Reform would divert all funds associated with Net Zero to fixing potholes and proving actual infrastructure.

I want to see a greater sense of pride in our villages, with thriving small businesses, shops, cafe

I am standing for the Liberal Democrats to offer real choice and much needed change to the electors of Liphook, Headley and Grayshott.  The Liberal Democrats are ready to offer a fresh approach.  The Conservatives have been in control of Hampshire County Council for 28 years and have taken your vote for granted.

I have the experience and commitment required to be a successful County Councillor:

**Leadership** I have spent my career running complex organisations under financial pressure.  As an experienced CEO I bring focus and delivery - exactly what HCC needs as budgets tighten and Council Tax rises.

**Community Commitment** I currently volunteer for three organisations supporting people with mental health, alcohol and substance abuse issues.  Early support for these people matters and reflects Liberal Democrat values.

**Councillor experience** I have previous experience as a Parish Councillor with responsibilities including planning permission, highways, facility management and charities.

I want to work with our village communities to
1. Protect and fix our local environment
2. improve access to Social Care for adults and Special Educational Needs and Disability support for children and young people
3. Re-think Road Maintenance so that we repair more defects and not less

I left the Conservatives after nearly 50 years because they are no longer an inclusive party.  They have just been trying to mimic Reform UK.  By contrast, it is the Liberal Democrats who offer responsibility, tolerance and respect as the essential elements of a free, fair and open society, founded on strong local communities,

If you share these values, then I hope you will consider voting for me on May 7th.  Thank you, and I look forward to working hard for you and your families.

If elected, you can expect honesty, hard work, and a genuine commitment to our community.

I will:

- Stand up for every resident, not just the loudest voices
- Work collaboratively, but challenge decisions that harm our area
- Fight for a fairer system that reduces inequality
- Protect the environment for future generations
- Support practical solutions that improve lives today

I believe politics should be about people, about making sure everyone feels heard, valued, and supported. It should also be about responsibility: to each other, and to the natural world we depend on.

Our countryside, our communities, and our public services are too important to take for granted. I will do everything I can to protect them, to build a future that is greener, fairer, and more hopeful for everyone.

My plan with over 30 years experience of delivering for residents is to find and persuade the person who can deliver results. Not into performative publicity seeking, I am dealing with the pollution of the Loddon, a Hampshire chalk stream, by winning agreement to meet a senior manager of Thames Water.

The New Forest has been my home for over 35 years, and I love it. As a GP in Lymington for all those years as well as holding many clinical public service roles, I have frontline experience of what needs to change and improve in our local system. I am passionate about finding sustainable solutions to enable the NHS to deliver quality care for all.



I am committed to addressing social and health inequalities; we have areas of deep deprivation in this constituency, sadly the issues of the vulnerable are often less well addressed in areas of affluence.



If I win your vote and become elected as your MP, I will:



Address the issue of lack of affordable housing in the New Forest

Be focussed on rebuilding our public services based on sound economic foundations

Be invested in minimising the effects of climate change and in protecting the environment

Help the young and vulnerable avoid falling into illegal activities including County Lines

Push for robust mental health support across the New Forest, currently tragically under resourced

Work hard on providing more accessible help to victims of domestic violence and abusive relationships



As your MP I will be approachable and commit to working hard to ensure we have a caring, inclusive and flourishing constituency.

I am a transplant surgeon with over 35 years’ experience in the NHS, and have lived in the New Forest for over 10 years. My time is now divided between helping out in transplant units in the UK, and working overseas in conflict zones such as Gaza. I have previously led negotiations on behalf of the BMA for both junior and senior doctors, successfully introducing new contracts for both groups. As a councillor, I would be committed to working hard to improve local services and encourage implementation of Green policies, such as improving public transport. I will actively seek the views of all those I represent throughout my tenure, not only prior to election.

I work in the housing development sector and have a background in commercial property where I gained my experience in Central London. With housing being a matter with an acute shortage of affordable property I have ideas on delivering homes for local people at costs which they can meet.

Hampshire County Council has an impressive property portfolio and my ambition would be to review its assets and explore ways to improve the capital it can generate where surplus revenue can make a meaningful contribution to improving the local infrastructure, such as the ongoing challenge of potholes.

The Hampshire Avon, a precious south coast Chalk Stream, is being tested beyond its capability to deal with the challenges that are presented to it and with my knowledge on how to improve outcomes to safeguard the interests of this truly valuable resource of natural capital and the many species that rely on our healthy rivers I hope to make a difference.

With some much of our County being owned by agriculture, I feel that it is imperative that we work with this sector to meet our environmental objectives. We need to build in resilience into our practices to safeguard ecology and ensure that there is an efficient mechanism for food delivery whilst considering the impacts and reducing these.

If elected as your councillor, my job will be to represent you. I will:

- Attend your Parish Council meetings to listen to the issues that affect you so that I can represent you and your community
- Always be available by phone, letter, email or in person, to listen to your problems with council services and do what I can to help you
- Provide regular updates on the issues I am working on for you

These are some of the important issues I am campaigning on:

**Stop the floods, fix the roads!**
The Council says it can’t afford to fix our roads. Yet flooding is a major cause of dangerous potholes. “We must deal with the flooding, so money is not wasted on repairs that don’t last.” Drains must be regularly maintained and the Council must use its powers to require landowners to clear their ditches. “I will campaign for a more proactive approach which will prevent damage and free up funds to do more repairs.”

**Restore our rural buses**
Many people rely on buses to get about and yet our services have been cut to the bone by the Conservatives. “I will fight to restore the Taxishare service, so no village is left without a lifeline to shops and GP surgeries.”

**Safer streets**
20 mph speed limits in some towns and villages would let people walk and cycle safely. In the forest, animals are being killed by speeding drivers. We need speed indicator signs to deter speeding, backed up by regular police checks.

**Make recycling easier**
“I will fight to keep our recycling centres open and campaign for community skips.” This will make it easier to dispose of large items and deter fly-tipping. We should also help people to recycle by accepting more items, such as foil, pots and tubs.

**Help for our high streets**
Our high streets must be places that people want to come to! We must work with landlords to encourage them to let empty shops. Lyndhurst and Fordingbridge must get their fair share of funding from the new Mayor’s investment fund. This will help to breathe new life in

Anne has represented Whiteley and Shedfield on Winchester City Council for the past four years. Anne brings her commercial background to her work with a determination to see local services improved. Her priorities have been to protect village communities while working to ensure that facilities are provided for the expanding Whiteley community to avoid pressure on nearby areas.

Resident in Meon Valley for over 20 years, Malcolm is an engineer with many years’ experience leading large, multi-national industrial projects. Malcolm values the special qualities of the natural environment of the Meon Valley and will always seek to protect them. Addressing the climate emergency is both a global and a local responsibility that will require strengthened communities, bringing back services and local businesses. 

As a current Winchester City Councillor, he has worked hard to speak up for our area including successfully combatting over-development in our villages, protecting our local rivers and waterways and improved accessibility for our residents. He is looking forward to achieve even more for the residents on Hampshire County Council.

Hampshire County Council provides local services like schools, roads and social services. Our area (Meon Valley) has had a Conservative County Councillor for more than 40 years. Many people think it’s time for a change.

Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace came second to the Conservatives in the Hampshire County Election last time. Since then, the Green vote has grown massively across the Meon Valley, including winning two seats on Winchester Council with 63% last year! If this year’s County by-election was based on how residents voted in last year’s local elections, then the GREENS WOULD WIN.

For a strong, independent voice who will make a real difference at Hampshire County, vote for Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace!

Martyn has worked in education in various roles, including college principal and now as an education consultant. He joined the Labour Party because he believes in equality, justice and fairness. Martyn has been Labour Party Secretary and Chair and was a councillor for Bridgemary North. He wants to represent all residents of Bridgemary and fight for the distinctive nature of the community. He believes that Gosport has been wrecked by the Conservatives: local democracy has been damaged by weak leadership and a lack of vision. Only Labour has the vision and the ability to turn things around.

I am a Town and District Councillor for Barton on Sea and New Milton where I’ve lived for over 45years. 
Ex Mayor of New Milton
Ex Chairman of New Forest District Council 
Treasurer and joint founder of Milton Heritage Society

**About Me**
I live in New Milton with my two children who attend local schools. I work for a family business in the New Forest aimed at connecting people with our beautiful natural environment.

I help out where I can: whether listening to children read at the local school, fundraising for the PTA, or gardening at the Scout hut. Now I want to do more as a county councillor.

My skills and experience will enable me to be an effective councillor. I have a degree in social policy & public sector management and have worked in the public sector.  I bring lived experience of the housing crisis - having been evicted under a section 21 order - and of the district’s pothole-riddled roads - having faced being stranded and the cost of replacing tyres.

**As a county councillor for New Milton**
- I will be a genuine community voice, listening to local residents, taking action and celebrating everything that makes our area exceptional.
- I will champion social justice, children’s education and social care reform. 
- I will ensure the environmental impact of council decisions are considered.

**My Focus will be**
Affordable Housing 
Sustainable Transport 
Cost of Living
Clean Rivers 
Improving Social Care

It was a great pleasure to recently return to Lymington to live after living in the Midlands being active in the community including as a district councillor.

I have a working background in the cultural sector. I have a keen interest in promoting culture as a positive way of improving and enriching lives.

My main focus will be on green issues and rejuvenating our wonderful high street. We need to find new ways to revitalise our town centres.

I believe in cross-party working and community consultation. There is too much political squabbling in the New Forest. As a councillor, I will offer experience and a desire to break through these barriers to produce effective local government.

I am standing once more as your candidate for New Milton North, Milford and Hordle Division in the Hampshire County Council elections on Thursday 7th May 2026.

It has been a privilege to be your Hampshire county councillor for the past 8 years. My priorities, if re-elected, will be:

* to be visible, available and responsive when you need my advice or representation regarding any county council matter, as I have always been for 8 years
* to develop projects that improve outcomes for residents, such as I did with the New Milton Men's Shed and local Chat About friendship programme
* to work diligently with county staff and colleagues to resolve your local issues
* to speak up for my division in wider county council decision-making processes
* to vigorously oppose excessive Local Plan development proposals for this beautiful rural area

A Conservative administration at Hampshire County Council will:

* ensure you get the best value services for the council tax you pay
* ensure prudent and effective management of the council's finances given the increasingly challenging pressures on services
* ensure a smooth transition to your new unitary council, making sure important services continue for you and your family, maintaining quality and with the least disruption possible
* tell you the truth about what can and cannot be achieved given tight budgetary constraints
* be totally focused on local issues and the care of local residents

This election is not about national politics or personalities. It is all about making sure our area has steady, reliable local leadership and choosing a councillor who knows the area and cares about you and your family. I therefore ask you to support me with your vote once more on May 7th 2026.

You can read more about what I have already achieved as your councillor and what I am determined to do in the next four years at https://francarpenter.com/

Kind regards,

Fran Carpenter 
Conservative Candidate, New Milton North, Milford & Hordle Divis

Aidan is standing to represent New Milton North, Milford and Hordle on Hampshire County Council.

A Chartered Surveyor with a distinguished career in professional standards and governance, Aidan would bring high-level strategic experience to the role. His background includes serving as a Foundation College Governor and a Trustee for housing and dementia charities, giving him a deep understanding of the vital services our communities rely on.

Aidan is committed to delivering results on three key issues: tackling the pothole plague with professional oversight, addressing residential speeding to keep our streets safe, and stopping sewage dumping in our local waterways.

Active in EcoSway and the coast2forest sustainable transport group, Clare is working to build a better-integrated, greener transport network across the New Forest - reducing car dependency and tackling the rural isolation that affects so many residents.

Having experienced being a parent with a child with additional needs and had a Mother who suffered with Dementia and Alzheimer's, Clare recognises the need to support people.

She is motivated by growing inequality, the damage done to the natural world, and her belief that disadvantaged people should not be further disadvantaged by decisions made at county level.

Philippa Gray is your local Liberal Democrat candidate for the Bedhampton Ward, where she has lived for over 30 years.

Philippa says, ‘I am inspired by the way that resilient communities can work together and want to use my energy, time and experience to help make Havant and Hampshire a better place in which to live, work and relax.’

Philippa enjoys taking practical steps to build our community and relieve the impact of climate change. She is an accredited Green Liberal Democrat, volunteers at Repair Café Havant and Solent Cycle Project and supports Havant Friends of the Earth and Havant Climate Alliance. Philippa is retired from work as an IT project manager but now works hard and uses her skills as a trustee of Bedhampton Community Centre and a director of Solent Remade Community Interest Company.

In her free time, Philippa loves growing and cooking vegetables, being a member of Havant U3A, trying to crochet and reading books.

I’ve lived in Havant since the mid 1980s and in West Leigh since 2007. I have been active in Leigh Park and West Leigh. I founded the Havant Community Litter Pickers over in 2018. We regularly clear the green by Sharps Copse, the woods by Havant Academy, Great Copse Woods off High Lawn Way, Petersfield Road and other areas. 

I have been a regular runner and volunteer at Havant Parkrun at Staunton Country Park, having volunteered more than 350 times and run over 500 times.

What can I do for you if you elect me? Work to Tackle Antisocial Behaviour in Leigh Park – I will campaign for CCTV in Leigh Park, better community policing, and cooperation between the police and the community they serve. We need more officers on patrol and better community policing where the concerns of residents are listened to and acted on.

Improved Services – New housing puts pressure on services such as GP’s surgeries, dentists, and schools. Improved services create employment in the area. I will work to ensure that Leigh Park is no longer neglected by the council and to promote the area as a great place to live and work.

Affordable Housing and Social Housing – We need affordable housing in Leigh Park. We particularly need social housing. Having a waiting list of a decade or more for a council house does not serve the community at all. Having housing that is sold outside of the wages of residents does not help people who live here. We need genuinely affordable housing to ensure that families are comfortable and suit resident’s needs.

Improve Measures to Prevent Climate Change – Not only do the green spaces in Leigh Park reduce pollution and give people places to walk and exercise but they make the town an enjoyable place to live. We need more recycling points and better rubbish clearance. I will work to introduce a community skip that will be placed in key locations for a short time each month to encourage people to throw things out rather than fly tip. This will help to protect our gree

I have been selected as the Conservative candidate for Hampshire County Council elections in May 2026, covering the Odiham and Hook Division.
I want to ensure that Hampshire continues to deliver great services and value for money.  I also wish to use my experience on both District and County to guides our organisations though Local Government Reorganisation.
Professionally: I spent 5 years Royal Navy, 22 years at Farnborough building spacecraft, growing the business and 13 years as an independent satellite contractor and consultant.
Locally: I have been a Hart District Cllr for 7 years, serving on the Oversight and Scrutiny and Development Management Committees, I am currently Chairman of the Council.  I also sit on the Farnborough Aerodrome Consultative Committee, and am the Hart Armed Forces Champion.  Previously I spent 12 years  on Crondall Parish Council (7 years as Chairman) accomplished many significant projects.  
I have 2 teenage sons.

**I am a local volunteer and have been chosen as Hart Lib Dems’ candidate for Hampshire County Council’s Odiham & Hook Division, and Hart District Council’s Hook Ward**.

I and my husband Tim have lived in Hook for over 30 years, and our son attended Hook and Robert Mays schools. I have experienced both the vibrant and supportive nature of the local community, as well as its increase in its size.

Our daughter Lizzie was born in 2000 with a rare genetic condition, and the 26 years since have been a huge learning curve for me in the SEND (Special Educational Needs and Disabilities) world.

In 2004, I became a parent governor of Lizzie’s SEND school, serving as Chair of Governors between 2016 and 2021, before finally retiring as a governor last year.

I also signed up to serve as a Parent Representative with Hampshire Parent Carer Network, a support forum and service provider for parents in similar circumstances.

Notably, Lizzie’s disability encouraged me to become a Parent Trustee and fundraiser with local charity Disability Challengers. For my day job, I am a medical statistician. This has given me a great respect for the value of data and robust evidence, and I would make sure that any actions I take as county councillor would always be based on the best possible evidence.

Hampshire needs to support local businesses and protect jobs as part of our wider plan to address the climate emergency.
I want to stand up for residents at Hampshire County Council and fight for better infrastructure to support the ever-growing number of new housing developments.  I want to speed up Hampshire’s action on climate change and improve the dreadful record on recycling.  I want a caring, better-run Hampshire that does not just cut services as its first reaction to a financial squeeze.

Rob Mocatta has lived in rural East Meon for 27 years. I am married with three grown up children. 3. Fight for Petersfield Butser on all council procurement and infrastructure decisions.

My name is Nick Drew. I live with my family in Froxfield and I have lived in the Petersfield and Sheet area for over 25 years. Despite the Labour government cutting £40m of funding, Hampshire continues to be one of the lowest tax councils in England. This is so important as the cost of living is so high these days; but we also have to continue providing high-quality, essential services. Our families need great schools, healthcare, homes and a continued safe place to grow. As your prospective councillor I will apply all my energies to attaining greater government focus and funding for you, the residents of East Hampshire.

I have led and managed very successful businesses both overseas and in the UK for many years and therefore I strongly believe I have the transferable skills to make a positive difference to the way local government is managed.

With an annual Budget £3.3 Billion Hampshire County Council needs more than ever stronger governance focused on educing its cost base, reducing waste, eking out efficiencies, and improving productivity across all areas to improve services to the community.

Now is the time to remove the established parties from Government and replace them with Reform who will make these changes.

Solar For Our Schools: Championing funding and community fundraising to install solar panels on schools.

Cleaning Our Communities: Launching public awareness campaigns and using legal avenues to pressure water companies to stop polluting our environment.

Reducing Our Bills: Creating a unified network (like a website or brand) to help community groups collaborate and better support residents with rising costs.

Finding Our Champions: Identifying and empowering local “silent heroes” with greater resources and public backing to strengthen the community.

As a private mental health therapist, Antonia helps clients locally and abroad with psychological issues, anxiety, addictions, unwanted behaviours, phobias and lack of confidence.  She works part-time in parish administration for local churches.

Long time member of Havant Green party, Dan is standing in this election to give Purbook and South Stakes voters the opportunity to show how important it is that we act on climate and nature. Roads are a clear priority. This winter has caused significant damage, and I share residents’ frustration. This election is about local leadership, not national politics. It is about who you trust to deliver services, manage your money responsibly, and stand up for our area.  I hope I can count on your support to continue doing so for Romsey Rural.
