The son of a Royal Marine, Bruce was widely travelled in his formative years.  As a consequence, he studied at ten schools before settling down to study at Eastney Modern for Boys, Portsmouth.

Bruce started his career at Portsmouth Dockyard as an apprentice Laboratory Mechanic. Four years later after leaving school without a single O level he had gained sufficient qualifications to go on to study at honours level an Electrical & Electronic Engineering Sandwich degree course at Portsmouth Polytechnic and from there worked in large scale manufacturing around the country, where his highlight was working in an aerospace & automotive factory in Paris with 1,200 French colleagues. Bruce's role at this facility was technical lead for the UK sales office for which learning to speak French was essential.

Years later his manufacturing career effectively came to an end when British American Tobacco closed its manufacturing facility in Southampton due to a change in government legislation. However by then Bruce had gained a merit pass in a Master of Business Administration postgraduate degree at the University of Southampton.

With his manufacturing career firmly behind him, Bruce turned his attention to politics, where in 2006 he became a Councillor at his first attempt in Eastleigh. By 2011, Bruce had gained another postgraduate degree, this time a merit pass in a Master of Public Administration at the University of Portsmouth and serves on four local councils including Hampshire County Council.

His most recent highlight was being invited by the Chinese government to give lectures on British democracy at several universities in southern China. He strongly supports education and manufacturing, and plans to use his influence to encourage Chinese students to study at British universities. To this end Bruce has enrolled on a Mandarin course at Southampton University.

🙋‍♂️ Introducing Myself — Steve Humby, Reform UK Candidate for West End South

Hi everyone! I'm Steve Humby, and I'm standing as your Reform UK candidate for West End South on Eastleigh Borough Council and Hampshire County Council at the May 7th local elections.

I live in Hamble and have proudly called this corner of Hampshire home for 38 years. You might know me from my time serving the community — I spent 31 years as a Retained Firefighter and 8 years as a Wholetime Firefighter with Hampshire & IOW Fire and Rescue Service. I also served as a Special Constable based at Bitterne and Park Gate, and as an Ambulance Co-Responder. Public service has always been at the heart of who I am.

So what's brought me to politics? Honestly — frustration. A recent experience dealing with my Parish Council opened my eyes to just how painful and unnecessarily bureaucratic local government can be. If it felt that way to me, I can only imagine how it feels for other ordinary residents trying to get things done. That has to change.

If elected, my priorities will be:
✅ Tackling Eastleigh's growing council debt
✅ Helping ease the cost of living for local families
✅ Simply working for the people of West End — not for the system

I'm not a career politician. I'm a retired firefighter who has spent nearly four decades living and serving in this community. I'd love your support — and more than that, I'd love to hear what matters most to you.

👍 Follow my Facebook page to keep up with my campaign, and feel free to drop me a message anytime. Let's work together for West End — vote Steve Humby on May 7th!

#ReformUK #WestEndSouth #Eastleigh #Hampshire #StepUpForWestEnd #VoteSteveHumby

My name is Ben Parry and I have lived with my family in Fair Oak for over 13 years. The Covid pandemic, and the climate crisis, have shown us that governance at any level cannot continue to be ‘business as usual’. It has also emphasised the importance of a strong community. My children attend a junior and secondary school in the local area. Until recently, I was a teacher for 17 years; teaching in local secondary schools and in an Eastleigh sixth form college. Now, as a director of an Eastleigh-based traffic survey company, I see how heavy investment in local housing has not been matched by investment in proper infrastructure.  Most importantly, I would be an independent voice on Eastleigh Borough Council, able to speak up with your best interests at heart, not the best interests of a party.

I am delighted to be standing as your local Borough Councillor Candidate on the 2nd May.

Please vote for me so that I can work alongside you all to make Tadley North, Kingsclere and Baughurst a better place to live.

Having served as a local councillor, I know how local government works – and more importantly, how to make it work for you. I’m here to listen, to act, and to stand up for what matters most to our community.

Some say I’m a bit of a maverick – and that may be true – but I’ve always stood by my principles as a proud Conservative who puts people first.

I look forward to continuing the conversation with you.

I have lived in Eastleigh for over 35 years and currently have run my shop in Eastleigh High Street for over 5 years. I’ve seen our community struggle while the Council wastes money and ignores local people. I’m standing to fight for our small businesses and comunity, tackle antisocial behaviour, and protect our green spaces from overdevelopment

Ben is standing for election in May because he believes that more Green voices are needed to champion the needs of _all_ residents, and campaign for stronger environmental action.

Ben knows that Green priorities – on addressing inequalities and the cost of living, and the climate and environmental crises – reflect the priorities of most people in Southwick & Wickham, and Southern Parishes, offering hope instead of division or managed decline.

Ben has extensive experience researching social and employment issues, developing policy that positively impacts people’s lives. He will bring these skills to his work as a district and county councillor, focusing on developing a dialogue with residents to understand their needs and to campaign for positive change. He will bring residents into the heart of the conversation and help them take action alongside him, coming together to get more involved in the politics that really mean something, not just social media outrage or pointing at potholes.

I am a full time mum and volunteer and I currently live in Winchester. I moved to Hampshire 10 years ago, after studying Sociology and Education and completing a PhD at Cardiff University. I have previously worked as a research assistant on various projects for universities and third sector organisations.

During the pandemic, when I stepped back from work to help my children learn at home, I got involved in fundraising for my children's school and became a parent governor. I also help supervise children on school trips.

When I talk to other school governors from across Hampshire at training events, I hear a lot of the same desperate stories. Parents are waiting years for suitable school places, children and young people cannot access mental health support, budgets are squeezed and headteachers are struggling to recruit and retain the quality staff they need to tackle educational disadvantage across the county. Schools are literally falling apart and it is no wonder when we have had six Tory Education Secretaries in the last five years.

We need a Labour government to put an end to this chaos and remove barriers to opportunity. The last Labour government played a pivotal role in raising aspirations for people from working class families like myself. I am therefore absolutely honoured to be chosen as the Labour candidate for East Hampshire.

Trevor Lloyd-Jones went to school and grew up in Yateley in Northeast Hampshire from childhood. Former school governor specialising in data, education KPIs and performance, he has experience managing small and very large businesses with a lifetime career in publishing, marketing, and the digital and technology industries. Former Conservative and former Conservative candidate Trevor is standing on a true reform agenda and a ‘small c’ conservative agenda: reform of the NHS, the judiciary, tax, with a pro-growth economic plan, and a new deal for young people on student debt and on home ownership.

Trevor is campaigning against cuts to the armed forces, the hollowing out of Britain’s defence sector and this government’s shoddy treatment of veterans. He was one of the Brexit Party candidates who stood aside for the Boris Johnson intake of Conservatives at the 2019 election.

In the media and locally Trevor is lobbying passionately for a new kind of bottom-up/bubble-up politics, an open, more ambitious and accessible politics. He speaks out against undemocratic and faceless global organisations taking the place of our democratic institutions.

But now, after more than a decade of the Conservatives the public have become conditioned to accept failure. They’ve become used to ministers blaming everyone but themselves, whether it’s the House of Lords, immigration lawyers, EU bureaucrats or the European Court of Human Rights. When not on the campaign trail Trevor can be found enjoying countryside walks or writing books on philosophy, politics and 'conscious capitalism'. He holds a degree in Economics and Public Administration from University of London and a Masters degree in Business Administration.
